title: Patal Bhuvaneshwar
tagline: Patal Bhuvaneshwar — heritage city of Uttarakhand

overview.short: Patal Bhuvaneshwar (Hindi : पाताल भुवनेश्वर) is a limestone Hindu cave temple 14 km from Gangolihat in the Pithoragarh district of Uttarakhand state in India.
overview.description: Patal Bhuvaneshwar (Hindi : पाताल भुवनेश्वर) is a limestone Hindu cave temple 14 km from Gangolihat in the Pithoragarh district of Uttarakhand state in India. It is located in the village of Bhubneshwar. Legend and folklore have it that this cave enshrines Lord Shiva and thirty three koti demigods [33 Types, In hindu neumerology "Koti" means "Types"]. The cave is 160 m long and 90 feet deep from the point of entrance.

topPlaces.name: Garur
topPlaces.description: Garur is a town in the Bageshwar district of the state of Uttarakhand, India.

topPlaces.name: Baijnath, Uttarakhand
topPlaces.description: Baijnath is a small town on the banks of the Gomati River in the Bageshwar district in Kumaon division of Uttarakhand, India. The place is most noted for its ancient temples, which have been recognized as Monuments of National Importance by the Archaeological Survey of India in Uttarakhand.

topPlaces.name: Baijnath Temple Complex
topPlaces.description: Baijnath Temple Complex is a cluster of 18 Hindu temples which are situated in the town of Baijnath in Uttarakhand, India. The complex is located in Bageshwar district along the banks of Gomati river at an elevation of 1,125 m (3,691 ft) above the mean sea level.

topPlaces.name: Kausani
topPlaces.description: Kausani (Kumaoni: Kôsānī) is a hill station and Village situated in Bageshwar district in the state of Uttarakhand, India. It is known for its scenery and its 300 km-wide panoramic view of Himalayan peaks like Trisul, Nanda Devi and Panchchuli.

items.note: Garur is a town in the Bageshwar district of the state of Uttarakhand, India.

Baijnath is a small town on the banks of the Gomati River in the Bageshwar district in Kumaon division of Uttarakhand, India. The place is most noted for its ancient temples, which have been recognized as Monuments of National Importance by the Archaeological Survey of India in Uttarakhand.

Baijnath Temple Complex is a cluster of 18 Hindu temples which are situated in the town of Baijnath in Uttarakhand, India. The complex is located in Bageshwar district along the banks of Gomati river at an elevation of 1,125 m (3,691 ft) above the mean sea level.

Kausani (Kumaoni: Kôsānī) is a hill station and Village situated in Bageshwar district in the state of Uttarakhand, India. It is known for its scenery and its 300 km-wide panoramic view of Himalayan peaks like Trisul, Nanda Devi and Panchchuli.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Patal Bhuvaneshwar?
faq.a: The best time to visit Patal Bhuvaneshwar is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Patal Bhuvaneshwar?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pantnagar Airport (98 km) and the nearest railway station is Kathgodam (Nainital railhead) (71 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Patal Bhuvaneshwar?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Patal Bhuvaneshwar at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Patal Bhuvaneshwar?
faq.a: Stays in Patal Bhuvaneshwar start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Patal Bhuvaneshwar?
faq.a: The top attractions are Garur, Baijnath, Uttarakhand, Baijnath Temple Complex, Kausani.
